answer:
Acme Analytics, Inc. reported total revenue of $12,600k for Q3 2026, a 16.5% quarter-over-quarter growth from $10,820k in Q2 2026. Growth was primarily driven by Latin America (38% QoQ growth) and Asia Pacific (24% QoQ growth), while North America remained the largest contributor at $4,200k. Monthly bookings showed an unbroken upward trend, increasing from $3,990k in July to $4,150k in September.

The principal risks identified are high supply chain concentration, medium FX exposure in Latin America, medium customer concentration, and low talent attrition in engineering.

The Platform product line was the primary revenue driver, contributing 68% of total revenue ($8,568k) with 18% QoQ growth. The company ended Q3 with 412 paying accounts, a net increase of 25, and a Net Revenue Retention (NRR) of 118%. The median contract value increased to $31k ARR.

Total headcount grew to 284, and operating expenses increased by 8% to $9,840k. The competitive win rate improved to 64%, with pricing and missing predictive analytics features being the most common reasons for losses. For Q4 2026, the company targets $14,200k in total bookings, a 12.7% sequential increase.
North America had the highest revenue in Q3 2026, with $4,200k.
A chart of revenue by region has been generated. The quarter-over-quarter revenue growth rate was 16.5%.
Yes, management views the overall trend as positive.

The top 3 risks listed in the report are: supply chain concentration (high severity), FX exposure in Latin America (medium severity), and customer concentration (medium severity).

Acme Analytics, Inc. experienced a positive Q3 2026 with total revenue of $12,600k, representing a 16.5% QoQ growth, led by Latin America and Asia Pacific, while North America remained the largest revenue contributor. Monthly bookings showed a consistent upward trend, and charts were generated to visualize regional revenue and monthly bookings, as well as the severity of the principal risks: supply chain concentration (high), FX exposure in Latin America (medium), and customer concentration (medium).
